steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two concerns
steps.content:
Both sentences support the same position—that televising should be resisted.

The first sentence gives one reason (participants would perform for cameras), and the second introduces another reason (viewers would watch only short clips).

The transition must therefore signal addition of another reason, not contrast or comparison.

steps.title: Match the transition to the logical relationship
steps.content: ”Additionally” correctly introduces another supporting reason. The other three would instead signal contradiction, comparison, or exemplification. This confirms B is correct.
